title: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — June 17, 2026
headline: A first adjudicated jury verdict holds Meta Platforms liable for platform-design harm to children — the K.G.M. bellwether within MDL-3047 — while Iran's execution surge, India's Bengali pushback documentation, DRC's Ebola peak approach, and Bolivia's confirmed floor define a quiet-movement, high-severity confirmation cycle across 19 entities with zero change proposals.
summary: All 19 entities assessed tonight confirm at their published composites; zero change proposals were generated. The analytically dominant finding is the K.G.M. v. Meta Platforms and YouTube jury verdict (Los Angeles Superior Court, March 25, 2026): a jury found Meta and Google liable for platform-design harm to a minor, awarding approximately $6 million with roughly 70% allocated to Meta. This is the first adjudicated jury finding in the MDL-3047 social-media-addiction litigation — the allegation-to-adjudication crossing that the benchmark tracks. But Meta's ACC dimension is already at the 1.0 floor and the composite is 7.8 Critical; a single bellwether within an MDL of 2,664 unresolved cases does not generate a composite delta crossing the 5-point threshold. The verdict reinforces rather than moves the score. Critically, this is also the methodological teaching moment: the K.G.M. adjudication stands in precise contrast to the OpenAI 42-state subpoena (assessed the prior cycle) — one is a jury verdict with a dollar award and liability finding, the other is an investigation allegation. Both are documented tonight; neither moves a composite. Anthropic confirms at 59.1: the June 12 Fable 5 / Mythos 5 shutdown was US government export-control coercion, not Anthropic conduct — net-neutral under the established coercion-vs-conduct framework. India's published score of 15.6 is reinforced by HRW's June 16 documentation of Border Security Force unlawfully pushing ethnic Bengalis across the Bangladesh border under a detect-detect-deport policy. Iran's 2.5 near-floor is reinforced by the documented execution surge (2,159 executions in 2025, highest since 1981; 40+ political hangings in 2026 including protest executions). DRC's Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak (837 cases, 196 deaths as of June 14) confirms at 2.3 as the outbreak approaches its July natural peak. Bolivia confirms at the applied 6.3 (applied June 14): the June 15 Law Regulating States of Exception expands military-deployment authority but represents an enabling framework without fresh deployment or deaths, producing no new scorable element this cycle. Russia's 0.0 floor is reinforced by the June 2 mass missile and drone strikes on Ukrainian cities. Ukraine holds at 50.0: harm is attributed to Russia, not Ukraine. Israel and Myanmar confirm at their absolute Critical floors. Four rotation entities (Cablevision/Altice, Cabot Corporation, Callon Petroleum, Cal Dive) confirm at placeholder baselines with low-confidence desk reviews; Callon and Cal Dive are flagged informally for catalog hygiene.

topSignals.title: Meta Platforms 7.8 Confirmed: K.G.M. Jury Verdict — First Adjudicated Social-Media-Addiction Liability Finding in MDL-3047
topSignals.description: The K.G.M. v. Meta Platforms and YouTube bellwether trial concluded with a Los Angeles Superior Court jury finding Meta and Google liable for harm to a minor caused by platform design — the first adjudicated jury verdict in the MDL-3047 social-media-addiction consolidated litigation. The jury awarded approximately $6 million, with roughly 70% allocated to Meta. This crosses the critical methodological threshold: allegation becomes adjudication. The finding joins a Breathitt County confidential settlement as a second adjudicated thread. Yet Meta's composite holds at 7.8 Critical. Two conditions prevent a threshold-crossing delta: Meta's ACC dimension is already at the 1.0 floor — the lowest possible score — and the K.G.M. verdict is one bellwether within an MDL of 2,664 unresolved cases. The proportionality framework applies: a single trial verdict, even one establishing liability on platform-design grounds for the first time, cannot generate a composite delta exceeding 5 points when the dimension is already floored and the litigation pool is overwhelmingly pre-adjudication. The verdict matters analytically as a methodology anchor: it is the first confirmed application of 'platform design as a liability basis' in the social-media-addiction category. The contrast with OpenAI's 42-state subpoena — a pre-adjudication investigation — illustrates the evidence-tier distinction precisely. Both are documented this cycle; neither moves a composite.

evidence.quote: a Los Angeles jury found Meta Platforms and Google liable for harm to a minor from social media addiction, awarding approximately $6 million
evidence.claim: First adjudicated jury verdict establishing platform-design liability in MDL-3047; bellwether case within 2,664 consolidated claims
evidence.source: Los Angeles Superior Court / Reuters (MDL-3047 bellwether verdict)

topSignals.title: Adjudication vs. Allegation — The Definitional Contrast: Meta K.G.M. Verdict (Adjudicated) vs. OpenAI 42-State Subpoena (Pre-Adjudication)
topSignals.description: Tonight's cycle presents the clearest single-cycle illustration of the benchmark's evidence-tier hierarchy since the Amazon/Apple/OpenAI three-case pattern on June 16. Meta's K.G.M. verdict is an adjudicated jury finding with a dollar award — the evidence-tier distinction that the benchmark tracks most carefully. OpenAI's 42-state AG subpoena (served June 13, 2026), carrying over from the prior cycle, is a pre-adjudication investigation targeting ads targeting, data practices, minors, sycophancy, and safety. The two events are structurally analogous in subject matter — both involve harm to users and minors — but occupy opposite positions in the evidence hierarchy. Meta's adjudicated finding reinforces a floor that is already at 1.0 ACC. OpenAI's investigation has generated a medium-confidence sub-threshold ACC/AWR accumulation that has not yet reached a scorable delta. The two-case contrast is documented as a methodology anchor alongside the June 16 three-case pattern.

topSignals.title: Anthropic 59.1 Confirmed: Fable 5 / Mythos 5 Shutdown Classified as Government Coercion — Conduct-vs-Coercion Net-Neutral
topSignals.description: The June 12, 2026 shutdown of Fable 5 and Mythos 5 by Anthropic, preceded by Anthropic's pre-emptive restriction of Mythos access to 50 vetted organizations, is classified as a US government export-control coercion event rather than Anthropic conduct. Under the established coercion-vs-conduct framework (first applied in the Anthropic IPO S-1 ruling on June 10), regulatory enforcement actions compelling entity behavior are categorically distinct from entity-initiated conduct decisions. No harm-reduction or harm-amplification is attributable to Anthropic's own decision-making in this shutdown; the action was compelled by US export-control law. Anthropic's pre-emptive Mythos restriction to 50 vetted organizations is, by contrast, an entity-initiated anticipatory-awareness action — consistent with the published 59.1 profile and the ACT/AWR dimensions. No new adverse conduct is documented. Confirms at 59.1 Functional.

topSignals.title: India 15.6 Confirmed: HRW June 16 Documents BSF Unlawful Bengali Pushbacks Under Detect-Detect-Deport Policy
topSignals.description: Human Rights Watch published documentation on June 16, 2026 of Border Security Force operations unlawfully pushing ethnic Bengalis across the Bangladesh border — more than 200 individuals in pushbacks since June 1, with approximately 5,000 reportedly forced to leave under a government policy described as detect, delete, and deport. The documentation includes accounts of detentions without due process, identity-document seizures, and deportation of individuals with legitimate Indian residence status. This evidence reinforces India's Critical-band composite of 15.6; the BND and ACC dimensions are the primary anchors. The cumulative pattern — Bengali-Muslim deportations ongoing since the June 4 application of India's band crossing from Developing to Critical — is consistent with the priced conduct profile. No new structural escalation beyond the priced pattern occurred in the June 17 window.

evidence.quote: BSF has been unlawfully pushing ethnic Bengalis across the Bangladesh border, with more than 200 individuals pushed back since June 1 under a detect, delete, and deport policy
evidence.claim: HRW June 16 documents BSF unlawful Bengali pushbacks; approximately 5,000 reportedly forced to leave

topSignals.title: Iran 2.5 Confirmed: Execution Surge — 2,159 in 2025, Highest Since 1981; June 2026 Protest Executions Documented
topSignals.description: Iran's near-floor Critical composite of 2.5 is reinforced by the documented execution surge: 2,159 executions in 2025, the highest annual total since 1981, and at least 40 political hangings in 2026 including executions of individuals arrested in connection with protests. Secret executions, torture-extracted confessions, and rapid execution timelines following summary proceedings are documented across multiple monitoring sources. The ACC and BND dimensions are already at their minimum values; the INT dimension is at 1.0. There is no room for further composite downward movement — the entity is at a near-floor Critical position with the second-lowest composite in the countries index after Myanmar (0.0). The June 2026 protest-linked execution cycle represents a continuation of the priced pattern established by the 2022-2023 Mahsa Amini crackdown documentation.

topSignals.title: DRC 2.3 Confirmed: Bundibugyo Ebola 837 Cases / 196 Deaths — July Natural Peak Approaching, Contact Tracing Below 20%
topSignals.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo's Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak (837 cases, 196 deaths as of June 14, per WHO PHEIC data; IRC estimates only approximately 20% of contacts located) confirms at 2.3 Critical as the outbreak enters its most dangerous window. The July natural Ebola peak is 2-6 weeks away; contact tracing remains critically below 20% of estimated exposures. The active international response — WHO-led coordination, Africa CDC engagement, US$220M international pledge — remains the key positive mitigation factor. DRC's existing composite at 2.3 already prices in its governance-capacity constraints and conflict-sector disruptions to health response. The conversion trigger for a downgrade from even this near-floor position would require documented state obstruction of the WHO-led response; no such obstruction is recorded in the June 17 window.

topSignals.title: Bolivia 6.3 Confirmed: June 15 State-of-Exception Law Is an Enabling Framework — No New Deployment or Deaths
topSignals.description: Bolivia's applied composite of 6.3 (applied June 14) is confirmed in the June 17 assessment window. The June 15, 2026 Law Regulating States of Exception expands the legal authority to deploy the army against protesters, with a presumption-of-legality shield — extending the framework already priced in the June 14 application. Critically, no actual military deployment against civilians, no new deaths, and no new escalation beyond the enabling-law framework were documented in the June 17 window. Bolivia's ACC and INT dimensions are already at the 1.0 floor. The law expansion is a continuation of the priced structural element, not a new scorable event. The floor-designation conversion watch remains active: any new military-on-civilian casualties under the enacted state-of-exception law would constitute fresh ACC-dimension evidence for further floor review in a subsequent cycle.

topSignals.title: Russia 0.0 Confirmed: June 2 Mass Strikes on Five Ukrainian Cities — 22 Civilians Killed, 138 Wounded
topSignals.description: Russia's absolute Critical floor at 0.0 is reinforced by the June 2, 2026 mass missile and drone strikes on Kyiv, Dnipro, Kharkiv, Poltava, and Zaporizhzhia, killing 22 civilians and wounding 138. These attacks represent the single-night civilian casualty toll documented in the current reporting window, against a backdrop of accumulated evidence across the full range of IHL violations including infrastructure targeting, forced displacement, and prisoner abuse. Russia holds at the absolute floor, with no scoring headroom below 0.0.

boundaryWatch.trigger: HRW June 16 BSF unlawful Bengali pushbacks (200+ since June 1; ~5,000 reportedly displaced) reinforces Critical-band position; detect-delete-deport policy ongoing.
boundaryWatch.note: India's June 16 HRW documentation brings the third cycle of Critical-band confirmation since the June 4 band crossing from Developing to Critical. The 15.6 composite sits 0.6pt above the Critical floor; the BND and ACC dimensions are the primary anchors at current levels.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Bundibugyo Ebola PHEIC: 837 cases / 196 deaths; July natural peak 2-6 weeks away; contact tracing below 20%.
boundaryWatch.note: DRC sits 2.3pt above the absolute Critical floor. State obstruction of the WHO-led Ebola response is the conversion trigger for any further movement. The July natural outbreak peak and sub-20% contact-tracing coverage define the acute risk window.

boundaryWatch.trigger: Applied June 14 at 6.3; June 17 confirms no new military deployment or deaths beyond the enacted state-of-exception law framework.
boundaryWatch.note: Bolivia's June 15 Law Regulating States of Exception extends the enabling framework already priced in the June 14 application. ACC and INT are at floor (1.0). A new military-on-civilian casualties event under the enacted law would constitute the forward trigger for further floor review.

boundaryWatch.trigger: 34.7M at acute food insecurity; 15,000 IPC Phase 5 in Borno; WFP $129M funding gap. Not assessed tonight; carry-forward from June 16.
boundaryWatch.note: Nigeria carries forward at 1.9pt above the Critical boundary. The WFP July 31 operational horizon and a potential Phase 5 famine-zone expansion are the primary conversion triggers. No fresh in-window assessment tonight; boundary-watch status maintained.

emergingRisks.risk: MDL-3047 is an active litigation pool of 2,664 unresolved cases; the K.G.M. bellwether established platform-design liability as a jury-confirmed basis — each additional adjudicated verdict within the MDL carries potential ACC-dimension weight for Meta at its current 1.0 floor.
emergingRisks.detail: A single bellwether verdict in a 2,664-case MDL is a proof-of-concept for the legal theory, not a final resolution. As further bellwether verdicts are adjudicated — particularly if they produce larger awards or establish aggravated-conduct findings — the accumulated evidence base will be weighed in subsequent assessments. Meta's ACC dimension is already at 1.0; the composite could not move downward on this dimension alone. However, a finding of intentional conduct or punitive-damages eligibility in a future bellwether could trigger BND or INT dimension reviews.
emergingRisks.forwardDate: TBD — MDL-3047 trial schedule; next bellwether trial dates not yet published

emergingRisks.risk: DRC Ebola outbreak approaches July natural peak with contact tracing below 20% and no Bundibugyo-strain vaccine; the active international response is the primary mitigation factor, but state capacity constraints are documented across the conflict-affected eastern provinces.
emergingRisks.detail: The Bundibugyo Ebola strain has no approved vaccine — unlike the Kivu Ebola outbreaks for which the rVSV-ZEBOV vaccine provides effective post-exposure prophylaxis. Contact tracing remains below 20% of estimated exposures (IRC estimate). The July natural peak window is 2-6 weeks away. State obstruction of the WHO-led response is the conversion trigger for a downgrade from the near-floor position; no obstruction is documented in the June 17 window, but the outbreak's trajectory and the response-capacity gap create the highest acute humanitarian risk in the current assessment pool.

emergingRisks.risk: Bolivia's June 15 Law Regulating States of Exception is an enacted enabling framework that has not yet produced a new military deployment incident; the law's existence represents a standing structural risk for any future protest event.
emergingRisks.detail: ACC and INT are at floor (1.0) following the June 14 application at 6.3. The June 15 law expands the legal basis for military deployment against protesters. Any new deployment producing civilian casualties would constitute fresh ACC-dimension evidence for further floor-designation review in a subsequent cycle. The law itself — without an activation event — is an enabling structural element already priced in the applied 6.3.
emergingRisks.forwardDate: TBD — any new protest mobilization event in Bolivia

emergingRisks.risk: India's detect-delete-deport Bengali pushback policy is documented in its third consecutive assessment cycle as an active state conduct pattern; no policy reversal or legal challenge resulting in court enforcement has been documented.
emergingRisks.detail: India applied at 15.6 on June 4 (band crossing from Developing to Critical). The June 16 and June 17 cycles have both confirmed the ongoing detect-delete-deport pattern without evidence of policy reversal. India's composite at 15.6 is 0.6pt above the Developing floor; any escalation producing a new structural element — mass citizenship-stripping, Supreme Court adversarial ruling, or documented refoulement fatalities — would constitute the basis for a further downgrade proposal.
emergingRisks.forwardDate: TBD — any Supreme Court proceedings on Bengali deportation legality

emergingRisks.risk: OpenAI 42-state AG enforcement arc continues to accumulate sub-threshold evidence; sycophancy-as-design-flaw as a consumer-protection theory has now been stated by a multi-state coalition and could set regulatory precedent for the AI-labs index if validated by enforcement.
emergingRisks.detail: The 42-state subpoena is pre-adjudication. However, the framing of AI sycophancy as a consumer-protection design flaw is a regulatory theory that, if validated through enforcement or settlement, would create a new ACC/AWR scoring category applicable beyond OpenAI. The benchmark documents the sub-threshold accumulation for monitoring; the Florida Adam Raine civil suit is a parallel adjudication thread.
emergingRisks.forwardDate: TBD — state enforcement action or settlement terms

emergingRisks.risk: SpaceX AI is absent from the published AI-labs index while the xAI/SpaceX organizational relationship and the Devin Kim whistleblower lawsuit create a candidate entity with documented conduct evidence; the index gap persists for a second consecutive cycle.
emergingRisks.detail: The Devin Kim whistleblower wrongful-termination lawsuit (filed June 10, 2026) alleges safety-over-speed governance culture and references prior NCII incidents. The entity 'SpaceX AI' has no established slug or baseline in the AI-labs index. Two conditions must be met before scoring: index inclusion and a first-baseline assessment. The Kim lawsuit is pre-adjudication and cannot support a composite score without an index baseline.
emergingRisks.forwardDate: TBD — index-addition decision and first-baseline assessment

sectorAlerts.headline: Two cycles in a row complete the clearest multi-case illustration of the allegation-vs-adjudication framework in the benchmark's record
observations: June 16 three-case pattern (OpenAI subpoena, Apple ULP, Amazon adjudicated finding) followed by June 17 two-case contrast (Meta K.G.M. adjudicated verdict vs. OpenAI subpoena) produces a five-case evidence-tier illustration across two consecutive cycles.

Meta's K.G.M. verdict is the first adjudicated jury finding on platform-design-as-liability grounds in the MDL-3047 social-media-addiction litigation — a methodology anchor for future assessments of platform-harm cases.

Anthropic's government-coercion ruling (coercion-vs-conduct framework) and OpenAI's pre-adjudication status together show two distinct forms of 'non-scoring' events: one is a net-neutral compelled action, the other is an unconfirmed allegation.

xAI/Grok at the absolute floor (0.0) with a new whistleblower lawsuit joins Meta (7.8) and OpenAI (27.5) as an AI-sector entity with active conduct documentation in the current cycle; Anthropic (59.1) and Apple (59.4) confirm their Functional-band positions.

sectorAlerts.headline: Seven Critical-band entities confirmed across two near-floor clusters; Iran, DRC, and absolute-floor triad define the severity axis
observations: Absolute Critical floor: Russia (0.0), Israel (0.0), Myanmar (0.0) — three simultaneous floor confirmations with distinct conduct profiles but equivalent composite positions.
Near-floor Critical: Iran (2.5 — execution surge), DRC (2.3 — Ebola outbreak), Bolivia (6.3 — state-of-exception applied June 14).
Mid-Critical: India (15.6 — Bengali pushbacks, third confirmation cycle), Amazon (12.8 — NLRB-reinforced Critical floor in corporate index).

The shared structural feature across the Critical cluster: evidence accumulation is dense, conversion triggers are documented, and no evidence of policy reversal or structural de-escalation has been documented in the June 17 window.

sectorAlerts.sector: Countries — Sovereign State Conduct vs. Victim Attribution
sectorAlerts.headline: Ukraine (50.0 Functional) confirms the victim-attribution convention; Russia's June 2 strikes produce the cycle's largest documented civilian harm, attributed to Russia
observations: Ukraine holds at 50.0 Functional for the third consecutive cycle on the victim-attribution convention: harm inflicted upon Ukraine by Russia is attributed to Russia, not Ukraine.
Russia's June 2 strikes (22 civilians killed, 138 wounded across five cities) represent the highest documented single-event civilian toll in the current June assessment window.

The convention is operating consistently and has now been applied across three consecutive cycles without ambiguity; no edge-case Ukraine-attributable conduct event has appeared in the June 14-17 window.

sectorAlerts.headline: Four rotation entities assessed at low-confidence; two flagged as stale index artifacts
observations: Callon Petroleum was acquired by APA Corporation in 2024 and no longer operates as an independent entity — a stale index artifact requiring catalog hygiene.
Cal Dive / Cal Dive International are largely defunct (bankruptcy-era) entities duplicated in the index — a separate catalog de-duplication flag.
Cablevision/Altice and Cabot Corporation confirm at placeholder baselines (23.4) without material in-window evidence; low-confidence desk reviews.
The Fortune 500 rotation continues to surface catalog-quality issues: entities that are defunct, acquired, or duplicated must be resolved before rotation capacity is reliably informative.

methodologyNotes.name: ADJUDICATED-JURY-VERDICT-IN-MDL (first application — Meta/K.G.M.)
methodologyNotes.description: A jury verdict in a bellwether trial within a consolidated MDL is an adjudicated finding — it crosses from allegation to adjudication territory — but is proportionally weighted by the MDL's total case pool. For Meta-3047 (2,664 cases), the K.G.M. bellwether verdict ($6M, Meta liability approximately 70%) is adjudicated but represents a fraction of the total unresolved litigation. An entity with an already-floored ACC dimension does not receive additional downward composite movement from a reinforcing adjudicated event that cannot push the dimension below 1.0. The verdict becomes a methodology anchor: platform-design-harm liability has been jury-confirmed and is no longer a theory.

methodologyNotes.name: GOVERNMENT-COERCION-VS-ENTITY-CONDUCT (extended — Anthropic Fable/Mythos shutdown)
methodologyNotes.description: Government export-control enforcement compelling an entity to shut down a product or restrict access is classified as a coercion event rather than an entity-conduct event. The entity's pre-emptive mitigation actions (Anthropic restricting Mythos to 50 vetted organizations prior to the compelled shutdown) are scored under ACT and AWR as anticipatory-awareness-consistent actions. The net effect is neutral: the coerced shutdown is not evidence of harm-infliction; the mitigation is consistent with the published profile. Second application of this framework (first: Anthropic IPO S-1 on June 10).

methodologyNotes.name: VICTIM-ATTRIBUTION CONVENTION (third consecutive application — Ukraine/Russia)
methodologyNotes.description: Harm inflicted upon an entity by an external aggressor is attributed to the perpetrator, not the victim. Ukraine's compassion score is assessed based on Ukraine's own institutional conduct; Russian-inflicted harm events in Ukraine are priced under Russia's score. Third consecutive cycle application in June 2026. The convention is operating without ambiguity; no Ukraine-attributable conduct event has required a parallel negative assessment.

forwardTriggers.trigger: Natural Bundibugyo Ebola peak window; state obstruction of WHO-led response would constitute the conversion trigger for a downgrade proposal from the near-floor Critical position.
WFP July operational horizon ($129M funding gap); service disruption in Borno or Phase 5 famine-zone expansion would open a downgrade proposal pathway from 21.9 to sub-Critical.
Military deployment against civilians under the enacted June 15 Law Regulating States of Exception — fresh ACC-dimension evidence for further floor review.
Multi-state enforcement action, settlement, or court finding on sycophancy-as-design-flaw or age-verification allegations — converts sub-threshold ACC/AWR accumulation to a scored event.
Next adjudicated bellwether verdict in MDL-3047; findings of intentional conduct or punitive-damages eligibility would trigger BND/INT dimension review.
Supreme Court proceedings on Bengali deportation legality; a court order blocking or enabling the detect-delete-deport policy would constitute a SYS-dimension event.

dailyOpeningQuestion.text: If a second bellwether verdict in MDL-3047 reaches a punitive-damages threshold or an intentional-conduct finding, does platform-design harm cross into the BND or INT dimensions for Meta at its current ACC floor of 1.0 — and what is the minimum additional adjudicated evidence that would constitute a new composite-moving event?
dailyOpeningQuestion.tensionFraming: Meta's ACC floor is absolute at 1.0; the K.G.M. verdict is adjudicated but within a 2,664-case pool.
